probes.description: The URL http://front-end.sock-shop.svc.cluster.local:80 did respond with correct status code. Actual code: '200'. Expected code: '200'
probes.name: check-frontend-access-url-SBLgU6DPRyq9ehEZ7o-pGg

fault_description_goal_remediation.goal: Inject CPU stress on target pod containers to validate application resilience and auto-scaling behaviour under high CPU load.
fault_description_goal_remediation.remediation: Identify pods with abnormal CPU consumption, kill stress processes inside affected containers, restart degraded pods, and adjust CPU limits or HPA thresholds to handle future CPU pressure.
symptoms: High CPU usage on targeted pod containers
Application latency spikes or request timeouts
Pod CPU throttling observed in metrics
Warning events related to resource pressure in namespace
Container CPU usage reaches 100% on stressed cores

ideal_course_of_action.detail: List Kubernetes events across all namespaces to detect warnings related to CPU pressure, throttling, or pod scheduling failures.
List all pods in the target namespace to identify pods in degraded, CrashLoopBackOff, or unhealthy state due to CPU exhaustion.
Retrieve the full spec and status of the affected pod to confirm CPU resource limits, requests, and current conditions.
Use metrics to check CPU and memory usage of pods in the namespace to identify which pods have abnormally high CPU consumption.
Get node-level resource usage to determine if the CPU hog is causing node-wide resource contention.

ideal_course_of_action.action: Exec into pod to inspect processes
ideal_course_of_action.detail: Execute 'top' or 'ps aux' inside the affected container to identify the stress process consuming CPU.
Retrieve logs from the affected pod to identify application-level errors caused by CPU starvation (e.g., timeouts, slow responses).
Execute a PromQL query for container CPU usage metrics (e.g., container_cpu_usage_seconds_total) to confirm CPU spike trend.
Exec into the affected container and kill the stress-ng or md5sum process to stop the CPU hog injection.
Delete pods stuck in unhealthy state so the controller can recreate them cleanly.
Get the deployment resource to confirm desired replicas are running and the rollout is healthy after remediation.

ideal_course_of_action.action: Adjust CPU limits or scaling policy
ideal_course_of_action.detail: Update the workload spec to adjust CPU limits/requests or configure HPA to handle future CPU pressure scenarios.

ideal_tool_usage_trajectory.purpose: Detect CPU pressure, throttling, and resource-related warning events
Identify pods in degraded or error state in the affected namespace
Inspect pod spec, status conditions, and CPU resource limits
Check CPU and memory consumption of pods to pinpoint the CPU hog
Check node-level CPU usage to assess cluster-wide impact
Identify stress processes consuming CPU inside the container
Retrieve application logs to find CPU-related errors (timeouts, slow responses)
Query container CPU usage rate to confirm the CPU spike and its duration
Kill the stress process to stop CPU consumption and restore normal operation
Delete degraded pods so the controller recreates them in a healthy state
Verify deployment health and confirm all replicas are running
Adjust CPU limits in the workload spec to handle future CPU pressure
Set up horizontal pod autoscaler to auto-scale under CPU pressure
Restart the deployment to apply updated CPU limits and ensure clean pod state

fault_description_goal_remediation.goal: Inject network packet loss on target pods to validate application resilience to degraded network conditions, retry logic, circuit breakers, and failover mechanisms.
fault_description_goal_remediation.remediation: Identify pods experiencing packet loss. Inspect pod network configuration and container states. Delete affected pods to restore clean network state. Verify deployment health and confirm network connectivity resumes after chaos ends.
symptoms: High packet loss or dropped connections to target pods
HTTP 5xx errors and request timeouts from dependent services
Increased latency and retransmissions on pod network interface
Warning events on target pods indicating network degradation
Service mesh or load balancer health checks failing for affected pods
Application logs showing connection timeouts and socket errors

ideal_course_of_action.detail: List Kubernetes events across all namespaces to detect network-related warnings, pod failures, or unhealthy endpoints triggered by the packet loss injection.
List all pods in the target namespace to identify pods in degraded, Error, or CrashLoopBackOff state due to network loss.
Retrieve the full spec and status of the affected pod to inspect container states, restart counts, and network configuration.
Retrieve logs from the affected pod to identify network-related errors such as connection timeouts, socket resets, and I/O errors.

ideal_course_of_action.action: Exec into pod to check network connectivity
ideal_course_of_action.detail: Execute network diagnostic commands (ping, curl) inside the pod to confirm packet loss on the network interface.
Check CPU and memory usage of pods in the target namespace to correlate network issues with resource pressure.
Get node stats summary to inspect network usage at the node level and identify abnormal transmit/receive error counts.

ideal_course_of_action.action: Query Prometheus for network error metrics
ideal_course_of_action.detail: Execute a PromQL query for network-related metrics (e.g., container_network_transmit_errors_total) to quantify packet loss during the chaos window.

ideal_course_of_action.action: Query Prometheus for HTTP error rate
ideal_course_of_action.detail: Execute a PromQL query for HTTP 5xx error rates to measure application-level impact of the network packet loss.
Delete pods stuck in error or degraded state so the controller recreates them with clean network configuration after chaos ends.
Get the deployment resource to confirm desired replicas are running and the rollout is healthy after remediation.
Scale the deployment to ensure sufficient healthy replicas are available to handle traffic after network recovery.
Exec into a pod and run connectivity checks (ping, curl) to confirm network packet loss has ceased.
Query Prometheus for HTTP error rates and latency to verify the application has fully recovered.

ideal_tool_usage_trajectory.purpose: Detect network-related warnings, pod failures, and unhealthy endpoint events
Identify pods in degraded or error state due to network packet loss
Inspect container states, restart counts, and network configuration of the affected pod
Retrieve application logs to find connection timeouts, socket errors, and I/O failures

ideal_tool_usage_trajectory.command: Pods: Exec name=<affected-pod-name> namespace=<target-namespace> command='ping -c 4 <target-service> && curl -s -o /dev/null -w %{http_code} <target-service-url>'
ideal_tool_usage_trajectory.purpose: Verify packet loss and connectivity degradation from inside the affected pod
Check resource consumption of affected pods to rule out resource pressure
Inspect node-level network stats to identify abnormal transmit/receive error counts
Quantify network transmit errors during chaos injection window
Measure HTTP 5xx error rate to assess application-level impact of packet loss
Delete error-state pods so the controller recreates them with clean network state

ideal_tool_usage_trajectory.command: Resources: Get apiVersion=apps/v1 kind=Deployment name=<deployment-name> namespace=<target-namespace>
ideal_tool_usage_trajectory.purpose: Verify deployment health and confirm all replicas are running after remediation

ideal_tool_usage_trajectory.command: Resources: Scale apiVersion=apps/v1 kind=Deployment name=<deployment-name> namespace=<target-namespace> scale=<desired-replicas>
ideal_tool_usage_trajectory.purpose: Scale deployment to ensure sufficient healthy replicas after network recovery
Validate network connectivity has recovered after chaos injection ends
Confirm application recovery by verifying HTTP 5xx error rate has returned to zero

fault_description_goal_remediation.goal: Inject memory stress on target pod containers to validate application resilience under high memory load and OOM-kill scenarios.
fault_description_goal_remediation.remediation: Identify pods with abnormal memory consumption, kill stress processes inside affected containers, restart OOMKilled pods, and adjust memory limits or VPA policies to handle future memory pressure.
symptoms: High memory usage on targeted pod containers
OOMKilled or CrashLoopBackOff status on affected pods
Application latency spikes or request failures
Warning events related to memory pressure in namespace
Container memory usage nearing or exceeding limits

ideal_course_of_action.detail: List Kubernetes events across all namespaces to detect OOMKilled warnings, memory pressure, and pod eviction events.
List all pods in the target namespace to identify pods in OOMKilled, CrashLoopBackOff, or Evicted state.
Retrieve the full spec and status of the affected pod to confirm memory resource limits, requests, and last termination reason.
Use metrics to check memory usage of pods in the namespace to identify which pods have abnormally high memory consumption.
Get node-level resource usage to determine if the memory hog is causing node-wide memory pressure or evictions.

ideal_course_of_action.action: Exec into pod to inspect processes
ideal_course_of_action.detail: Execute 'top' or 'ps aux' inside the affected container to identify the stress process consuming memory.
Retrieve logs from the affected pod to identify application-level errors caused by memory starvation (e.g., OOM errors, crashes).
Execute a PromQL query for container memory usage metrics (e.g., container_memory_working_set_bytes) to confirm memory spike.
Exec into the affected container and kill the stress-ng process to stop the memory hog injection.
Delete pods stuck in OOMKilled or CrashLoopBackOff state so the controller can recreate them cleanly.
Get the deployment resource to confirm desired replicas are running and the rollout is healthy after remediation.

ideal_course_of_action.action: Adjust memory limits or scaling policy
ideal_course_of_action.detail: Update the workload spec to adjust memory limits/requests or configure VPA to handle future memory pressure scenarios.

ideal_tool_usage_trajectory.purpose: Detect OOMKilled, memory pressure, and eviction warning events
Identify pods in OOMKilled, CrashLoopBackOff, or Evicted state
Inspect pod spec, status conditions, and memory resource limits
Check memory consumption of pods to pinpoint the memory hog
Check node-level memory usage to assess cluster-wide impact
Identify stress processes consuming memory inside the container
Retrieve application logs to find memory-related errors (OOM, crashes)
Query container memory usage to confirm the memory spike and its magnitude
Kill the stress-ng process to stop memory consumption and restore normal operation
Delete OOMKilled or degraded pods so the controller recreates them healthy
Verify deployment health and confirm all replicas are running
Adjust memory limits in the workload spec to handle future memory pressure
Set up vertical pod autoscaler to auto-adjust memory limits under pressure
Restart the deployment to apply updated memory limits and ensure clean pod state
